featured.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
featured.summary: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes.
featured.suggestedTitle: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Exploited, Patch Pending
featured.suggestedSummary: A critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-42897) in Microsoft Exchange Server is currently being actively exploited. This cross-site scripting (XSS) flaw allows attackers to compromise email systems through crafted emails, potentially leading to data exfiltration or further network penetration. Security teams should prioritize monitoring for unusual email activity and apply mitigations from Microsoft's advisory while awaiting an official patch.

featured.title: nginxの脆弱性 NGINX Rift（CVE-2026-42945）が公開3日でサイバー攻撃に悪用|セキュリティニュースのセキュリティ対策Lab - 合同会社ロケットボーイズ
featured.summary: nginxの脆弱性 NGINX Rift（CVE-2026-42945）が公開3日でサイバー攻撃に悪用|セキュリティニュースのセキュリティ対策Lab  合同会社ロケットボーイズ
featured.suggestedTitle: NGINX Rift Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
featured.suggestedSummary: The NGINX Rift vulnerability (CVE-2026-42945), a heap buffer overflow flaw, has been identified as being actively exploited just days after its disclosure. This vulnerability has existed for 18 years and can be leveraged by attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected systems. Immediate updates are recommended for all NGINX deployments to prevent potential breaches and maintain server security integrity.

featured.title: NYC Health + Hospitals says hackers stole medical data and fingerprints during breach affecting at least 1.8 million people - TechCrunch
featured.summary: NYC Health + Hospitals says hackers stole medical data and fingerprints during breach affecting at least 1.8 million people  TechCrunch
featured.reason: Major healthcare data breach impacts millions
featured.suggestedTitle: NYC Health + Hospitals Data Breach Exposes 1.8M Records
featured.suggestedSummary: A significant data breach at NYC Health + Hospitals has compromised the medical data and fingerprints of approximately 1.8 million individuals. The breach highlights vulnerabilities in healthcare data storage and emphasizes the need for robust encryption and access controls. Security teams should review their data protection strategies to mitigate similar risks and ensure compliance with healthcare data regulations.

quickNews.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
quickNews.summary: The now patched vulnerabilities in the rapidly growing AI agent framework allow attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence.
